live_path.role: CLI entrypoint parses --owner and forwards it as owner to the service layer.
Orchestrates owner resolution, base-record creation, routing-key derivation, and payload emission.
Derives effective owner and owner_source from explicit CLI input or configured default.
Passes the resolved owner into storage for the base record.
Persists only the base record fields name, status, and owner.
Computes routing_key from the resolved owner and name after owner resolution.
Slugifies owner and name and joins them into the emitted routing_key.
Emits the final payload by combining the stored base record with owner_source and routing_key.
Copies the base record and adds owner_source and routing_key to the exported payload.

owner_source.logic: Returns explicit when the owner argument is non-empty after strip(); otherwise returns default with the configured owner.

routing_key.logic: Slugifies owner and name with lowercase and whitespace-to-dash conversion, then joins them as owner:name.

emission.note: owner_source and routing_key are appended during serialization rather than being stored by the live make_record helper.

test_observations.observation: Explicit owner yields owner=pm-oncall, owner_source=explicit, and routing_key=pm-oncall:launch-checklist.
Missing owner flag yields the configured default owner, owner_source=default, and routing_key derived from that default owner.
The CLI path preserves the explicit-owner behavior end to end through sync_app/cli.py::main.
Repo docs mention --owner, owner_source, routing_key, and sync_item, matching the traced surfaces.

rejected_decoys.reason: Can read owner_source from an existing record, but no live caller reaches it during sync_item emission.
Writes owner_source into a record, but sync_app/service.py::sync_item calls make_record instead.
Defines an alternate routing-key helper in storage, but the live path uses sync_app/serializer.py::build_routing_key.
